message:
fix(messaging/fabric): include full handshake material in discovery session fingerprint

Previously, DiscoverySessionId::for_handshake() hashed only a subset of
the discovery hello fields (node IDs, bootstrap key, credential issuer,
membership epoch, signature) to produce the session identifier. This
meant that two handshakes with identical credentials but different
resource budgets, interest summaries, capability sets, stewardship
leases, control epochs, advisory hints, or local negotiation policies
would collide to the same session ID. A peer could reuse a credential
across materially different discovery contexts and receive the same
session-scoped blinding, defeating replay detection for non-credential
handshake fields.

Introduce DiscoverySessionFingerprintMaterial — a canonical struct that
captures all semantically meaningful fields from both the remote hello
and the local negotiation policy. The struct's fields are sorted and
normalized via helper functions (canonical_discovery_capability_keys,
canonical_discovery_interest_summary, etc.) to ensure deterministic
hashing regardless of input ordering.

DiscoverySessionId::for_handshake() now accepts the local policy and
hashes the full fingerprint material instead of ad-hoc tuple fields.
DiscoverySession::establish() is updated to pass the policy through.

Adds two regression tests:
- discovery_session_id_changes_when_handshake_material_changes_without_
  rotating_credential: verifies that changing resource_budget.repair_slots
  produces a distinct session ID even with an identical credential
- discovery_session_id_changes_when_local_negotiation_policy_changes:
  verifies that changing lease_ttl_millis in the local policy produces
  a distinct session ID

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
